content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN points (numbered 111 through NNN) in a Cartesian coordinate system. For each valid iii, the coordinates of the iii-th point are (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi​,yi​).

You should answer QQQ queries (numbered 111 through QQQ). For each valid iii, in the iii-th query:


You are given two points L=(li,0)L = (l_i, 0)L=(li​,0) and R=(ri,0)R = (r_i, 0)R=(ri​,0).
Consider an isosceles right triangle such that its hypothenuse is the line segment between the points LLL and RRR (two of its vertices) and the vertex opposite to it lies above the xxx-axis.
The answer to this query is the number of points among the given NNN points which lie inside or on the perimeter of this triangle.
constraints: 0≤xi,yi≤1060 \le x_i, y_i \le 10^60≤xi​,yi​≤106 for each valid iii
0≤li<ri≤1060 \le l_i \lt r_i \le 10^60≤li​<ri​≤106 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi​ and yiy_iyi​.
QQQ more lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers lil_ili​ and rir_iri​.
OuputFor each test case, print a single line containing QQQ space separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be the answer to the iii-th query.
